Feedback Text: The CEO reviewed our recent launch and said the UI was too 'blue'. They drew a sketch on a napkin of how it should look and told engineering to implement it by Monday.
We had to completely scrap the onboarding redesign we spent 3 weeks validating with users because the VP of Sales promised a custom Salesforce integration to a prospect to save a deal.
We are exhausted. We just ship whatever the highest paid person in the room asks for. No one asks 'why'. We are just order-takers.
I presented data showing that 80% of users don't want the new social feed feature. Leadership ignored the data and said we're building it anyway because our competitor has it.
The backlog is entirely dictated by sales promises and executive whims. We haven't looked at a user metric or outcome in 6 months. We just focus on shipping 50 story points a sprint no matter what.
Churn is spiking. Users are leaving because the platform is becoming bloated with features they don't use, while the core workflow remains broken.
We deployed 4 major features this month exactly on the deadline the CEO set. The app is slower and full of bugs, but leadership is celebrating the 'velocity'.
'The app keeps changing every week and it's impossible to use. Stop adding random features and fix the bugs!'
I tried to introduce an OKR around user retention, but was told our only goal for the year is 'Deliver the 25 features on the roadmap on time'.
What's the point of even doing retrospectives? If a VP wants something built, we drop everything and build it. We have zero autonomy or strategic direction.
